//: RENDERED, DO NOT EDIT — bin/steward-session.sh writes a per-fleet copy to ~/.config/claude-fleet/fleets/<session>/steward-settings.json at spawn (issue #284), substituting __DENY_BASE__ (this fleet's base checkout), __DENY_WORKTREES__ (its issue-worktree sibling glob) and __BIN__ (the install bin dir). Launched with `claude --settings <that copy>`. Edit the template here; it re-renders on the next hub respawn.
//rail: The no-code rail: the steward is a dispatcher, never a coder. deny Edit/Write across the fleet's base checkout AND its issue-<N> worktree siblings so a steward turn can never touch the fleet's code (that is a worker's job). NotebookEdit is denied outright. Path-scoped ON PURPOSE — the steward still Writes its own memory files (~/.claude/projects/*/memory) and scratchpad. deny overrides bypassPermissions (deny always wins), so the rail holds even though the fleet runs bypass; the PreToolUse hooks/base-readonly-guard.py backstop (issue #355) enforces the same base-checkout read-only rail for EVERY seat (notably the worker, which has no permissions.deny), and a Bash subprocess that writes via redirection/python is out of scope (accident rail, not a sandbox).
//allow: Pre-approve the steward's routine read-only command set so a non-bypass steward stops stalling on prompts — plus spawning (dash-issue-session.sh) and reaping (fleet-cleanup.sh), which ARE the steward's job. Redundant under bypassPermissions (everything is allowed there), harmless, and the whitelist the moment an operator runs the hub in a stricter mode.
